title: $5/9-$Magnetization Plateau and Spin Supersolidity in YCu$_3$(OD)$_{7-x}$Br$_{2+x}$ under Magnetic Fields up to 120~T
authors: Hiroaki Hayashi, Han Li, Feng-Feng Song, Xu-Guang Zhou, Akira Matsuo, Taeyun Kim, Enze Lv, Yuto Ishii, Zhe Qu, Gang Su, Koichi Kindo, Kwang-Yong Choi, Wei Li, Yasuhiro H. Matsuda
abstract: We performed high-precision magnetization measurements up to 120~T on three compositions of the newly discovered kagome antiferromagnet YCu$_3$(OD)$_{7-x}$Br$_{2+x}$ (YCOB), revealing a previously unobserved 5/9 fractional magnetization plateau. All YCOB samples with different Br$^-$ concentrations exhibit nearly identical magnetization curves below 60~T, whereas the 5/9 plateau appears at markedly different fields in the ultrahigh-field regime. By modeling the experimental data using tensor-network calculations, we derive the effective spin Hamiltonians for the YCOB family with three spatially anisotropic Heisenberg couplings (the 3$J$-type model), which quantitatively reproduces the measured magnetization processes and captures the composition-dependent evolution of the 5/9 plateau. Furthermore, our theoretical analysis suggests the emergence of a spin supersolid phase in the field window between the 1/3 and 5/9 plateaus, which is sensitive to spin exchange parameters and accounts for the significant variation in the critical fields of the 5/9 plateau observed among different YCOB compositions.
summary: This paper reports the discovery of a 5/9 magnetization plateau in the kagome antiferromagnet YCOB using ultrahigh magnetic fields up to 120 T. By applying tensor-network simulations, the authors demonstrate that an anisotropic 3J-type Heisenberg model explains the observed magnetization and predicts a spin supersolid phase.
main_problem: The study aims to determine the high-field magnetic behavior of the kagome antiferromagnet YCOB and investigate the existence of higher-order fractional magnetization plateaus and exotic phases like spin supersolidity.
main_result: The researchers discovered a previously unobserved 5/9 fractional magnetization plateau and identified a spin supersolid phase between the 1/3 and 5/9 plateaus using an anisotropic 3J-type Heisenberg model.
method: High-precision magnetization measurements were performed using the single-turn coil technique up to 120 T, and experimental data were modeled using DMRG and iPEPS tensor-network calculations.
model_or_system: The system is the kagome lattice antiferromagnet YCu3(OD)7-xBr2+x (YCOB) with tunable Br-concentration. The model is an effective 3J-type Heisenberg Hamiltonian with three spatially anisotropic exchange couplings (JR, JG, JB).
key_observables: Magnetization (M) as a function of magnetic field (H), differential susceptibility (dM/dH), and local spin expectation values.
important_parameters: Br-concentration (x), exchange couplings (JR, JG, JB), magnetic field up to 120 T, and the g-factor (~2.2).
main_assumptions: The system is modeled using a 3J-type Heisenberg model to account for lattice distortions caused by OH/Br site mixing.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows magnetization and susceptibility curves; Fig 4 shows calibration of high-field data; Fig 5 displays loss maps for DMRG and iPEPS parameter space; Fig 6 compares experimental M-H curves with numerical results.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the YCOB system, presents high-field experimental magnetization data, describes the 3J-type model and numerical fitting procedure, discusses the composition-dependent evolution of plateaus, and proposes the spin supersolid phase.
why_interesting: The discovery of a spin supersolid phase and the study of fractional magnetization plateaus in a frustrated kagome lattice provide deep insights into many-body dynamics and the emergence of exotic broken-symmetry phases in strongly correlated systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Magnetization process and dM/dH data of YCOB (x ≃0.50) measured at 2 K. Previous measurements up to 60 T [47] are denoted as purple symbols as a comparison, where a very good agreement is seen to the present measure- ments. The observed magnetization plateaus are labeled by the yellow shadow and black arrows. (b) Magnetization pro- cess for different compositions of YCOB (x ≃0.05, 0.50, and 0.95) measured at 2 K.

FIG. 2. (a) Experimental and DMRG results for the normalized magnetization curves of YCOB. The gray, red, and blue solid (dashed) curves represent the calculated (measured) data for Br compositions x ≃0.05, 0.50, and 0.95, respectively. The black curve corresponds to the ideal KHAF model. The exchange parameters, given in units of JB, are the best-fitting values obtained from the analysis described in the Supplemental Materials [58]. The inset illustrates the definition of the three exchange couplings JR, JG, and JB on the nonequivalent bonds. FIG. 3. (a) Many-body magnetization calculations for the YCOB material with x ≃0.50. The experimental data are shown as a red curve, the DMRG results are presented by the blue curve with the best-fit 3J parameter set, and the iPEPS results with the same parameter are shown with light blue color. (b) Schematic field-induced phase diagram. (c) Local moment ⟨Sz i ⟩on each site i calculated with DMRG method, with parameter set JR = 0.8, JG = 0.5, JB = 1, and a fixed magnetization of M/MS ≃4/9. The color intensity of the circle at each site i indicates the magnitude and sign of the value, indicated by the color bar on the right. (d) The average of local moment ⟨Sz⟩col (see main text).
